name: Turkey Mole
recipeIngredient: Coriander seeds, toasted and crushed (1/4 tsp)
Ground dried ancho or mixed chile peppers (2 tbsp)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a large, heavy-bottomed saute pan and brown turkey on all sides.
2. Remove turkey and set aside.
3. Add onion to the pan and saute until lightly browned.
4. Add garlic, sesame seeds, cinnamon, cloves, coriander, ground chile peppers, and chopped cilantro; cook over moderate heat for 5 minutes.
5. Add chicken stock and simmer for about 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
6. Pour the mixture into a blender or food processor and process until smooth.
7. Return sauce to the pan, add reserved turkey, and simmer, covered, until tender (about 35 minutes). Thin with additional stock if necessary. Season with salt and pepper.
8. Arrange turkey on a warm platter. Whisk the sauce until smooth and pour over turkey.
9. Garnish with roasted and julienned red bell peppers, warm tortillas, and fresh cilantro sprigs.
